summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a low-privilege user with only `credential:user` access to a shared credential can coerce `/rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options` into making an authenticated outbound request to an attacker-controlled endpoint and exfiltrating the bearer token.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should show that a read-only credential sharee can trigger an authenticated outbound request through dynamic load-options routing and recover the credential-derived `Authorization` header from an attacker-controlled capture endpoint.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned n8n at ref `n8n@2.25.1`, checked out commit `d1c8049ddb5dd8013cca746e450c5933b8403462`, built the Docker reproduction image, executed the bundled integration test, and produced `reproduction/last-run/capture.json` containing `Authorization: Bearer evohunt-secret-token` together with `reproduction/last-run/result.json` showing a 200 response from `/rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options`.
verification_method: The bundled Jest test provisions an owner-held Asana credential, shares it to a member account with only `credential:user` access, invokes `POST /rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options` with attacker-controlled routing, and verifies that the attacker capture server recorded the exact bearer token in `reproduction/last-run/capture.json` while the endpoint returned the expected success payload.
evidence_produced_by_run: reproduction/last-run/capture.json shows the attacker-controlled endpoint received `Authorization: Bearer evohunt-secret-token`.
reproduction/last-run/result.json records that the caller only had `credential:user` sharing and that `/rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options` returned HTTP 200 with the expected option payload.
reproduction/last-run/in-container.log shows the Dockerized build path and a passing Jest suite for the bundled exploit test.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref and commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
